title: Manchester United's new stadium to have fewer car parks

input.lead: Fans of Manchester United are being told to use public transport - to get to matches at their new 100,000 capacity stadium
input.summary: Fans of Manchester United are being told to use public transport - to get to matches at their new 100,000 capacity stadium
input.evidence_text: Supporters of Manchester United will be "nudged" into using public transport to travel to matches at the club's planned new 100,000 capacity stadium The club announced they want to build their new ground on the site of what is now an indoor go-karting track in Trafford Park, after they acquired the land earlier this year A strategic masterplan document, which has been launched for the Trafford Wharfside area, states: "Existing surface car parks must be consolidated and removed to make best use of available land" The leader of Trafford Council Tom Ross said "car reliance was something we need to work though" adding they needed to "nudge people towards public transport alternatives" The Trafford Wharfside Strategic Masterplan covers an area of 150 hectares, with much of the land currently dominated by surface car parks, which are used by Manchester United supporters on matchdays There are currently 11 official matchday car parks surrounding Old Trafford, with hundreds of spaces available to supporters, including those with blue badges

title: Bomb hoaxer who targeted Andy Burnham, MPs and judges sentenced

input.lead: Anthony McGrath was given a community order and told he must undergo mental health treatment
input.summary: Anthony McGrath was given a community order and told he must undergo mental health treatment
input.evidence_text: A bomb hoaxer claimed he would target a town hall after accusing Andy Burnham of "protecting paedophiles", and sent threats to professionals including two MPs Anthony McGrath, 57, posted on X in November last year threatening to bomb Greater Manchester Police headquarters unless they returned property belonging to him, Liverpool Crown Court heard In 2024 he sent threatening emails to Salford MP Rebecca Long-Bailey and Sir Robert Buckland, who at the time was the MP for South Swindon McGrath, of Sale, Greater Manchester, who pleaded guilty at an earlier hearing, was sentenced to a 15-month community order, given a seven-year restraining order, and ordered to have mental health treatment The court heard McGrath had been sectioned under the Mental Health Act following his arrest Martyn Walsh, prosecuting, said that in one email sent to Long-Bailey, McGrath said: "You all messed with the wrong person. My army is much bigger considering I have Russian and Chinese support. We will be armed and shoot to kill."

final_reason: Status: drop

title: What is a hosepipe ban, why are they needed and who decides them?

input.lead: If the heatwave is here to stay, could a hosepipe ban be introduced in the north-west of England?
input.summary: If the heatwave is here to stay, could a hosepipe ban be introduced in the north-west of England?
input.evidence_text: The latest heat-health alert has been in force across the north-west of England since Wednesday and will remain in place until 21:00 BST on Sunday. , external Last month a more severe - amber - warning of extreme heat was issued in our region as temperatures soared to record levels for June We are still relatively early in the summer, too, with our hottest weather normally coming in late July and August If the hot and dry weather continues, will talk of hosepipe bans be far away? A hosepipe ban, also known as a temporary use ban, is imposed by water companies to try to save water during periods of high demand or low supply In north-western England that responsibility falls to United Utilities (UU), which supplies three million households and 200,000 businesses

title: Coroner rules Nobby Stiles death should be subject to inquest

input.lead: An inquest into his death will be held after a corner finds injury consistent with repeated head traumas
input.summary: An inquest into his death will be held after a corner finds injury consistent with repeated head traumas
input.evidence_text: England World Cup winner Nobby Stiles died with a traumatic brain injury, a court heard as a coroner ruled an inquest into his death must be held Stiles, 78, a former Manchester United footballer and 1966 World Cup winner, died with chronic traumatic encephalopathy (CTE), which has been associated with head traumas claimed to be from repeatedly heading a football Chris Morris, area coroner for Greater Manchester South, told a court a full inquest was needed after a brain expert examined his medical records Stiles died in 2020, and his family has campaigned for football authorities to do more to help ex-players cope with injuries they claim were caused during their playing days Norbert "Nobby" Stiles, born in Manchester in 1942, was a tough-tackling defensive midfielder, capped 28 times by England and played nearly 400 times for Man Utd Mr Morris told Stockport coroner's court "for reasons not entirely clear to me" Stiles's death was not at the time reported to the coroner's office for investigation, which had only begun after information provided by the ex-footballer's family

title: Man who killed Middleton teen was in 'blind panic', court hears

input.lead: A man is on trial after Devon Simmonds-Caines, 15, died in Middleton in July 2025
input.summary: A man is on trial after Devon Simmonds-Caines, 15, died in Middleton in July 2025
input.evidence_text: Man who killed Middleton teen was in 'blind panic', court hears - BBC News Image source, GMP Image caption, Devon Simmonds-Caines, 15, died after being run over in Middleton in July 2025 By Phil Cunliffe , Reporting from Manchester Crown Court and Georgie Docker , North West Published 10 July 2026 A man who killed a teenager who was riding a stolen e-bike has told a court he "was just trying to get the bike back". Terrence King, 60, has admitted manslaughter but denies murdering Devon Simmonds-Caines in July 2025. The court heard King drove his Vauxhall Insignia at double the 20mph speed limit, before veering left into Devon without applying the brakes. Devon was 15 when he died from multiple injuries at the scene on Rowrah Crescent in Middleton, Greater Manchester. It is expected the jury will be sent out to consider its verdict on Monday. 'Serious injury' Taking the stand at Minshull St Crown Court, King explained how he saw Devon riding the £3,000 e-bike, which he had taken from another boy following a scuffle. King said he was angry and had told police there was "blind panic" as he sought to get the bike back. Under cross-examination, King said he had only intended to get alongside or behind Devon. "I saw the bike and decided to try and block it in," King said. Defending barrister Mukul Chawla KC asked King why he did not brake. "I don't know," said King. Chawla said: "Did you know Devon? Did you know whole stole the bike?" King replied: "No." It was revealed King refused to watch CCTV footage of the crash when it was played to him by the police the next day. "Why was that?" asked Mr Chawla. "Because Devon had passed away," said King, who denied intending to hurt the youngster. Jurors have been told King has previously pleaded guilty to manslaughter. title: Why voting method change matters in mayoral poll

input.lead: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.summary: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.evidence_text: The Labour Party could benefit most from the return of the Supplementary Votes (SV) system in the Greater Manchester mayoral race, a polling expert has said Voters in the region head to the polls on 30 July and will be able to select a first and second choice of candidate as SV comes into replace the first-past-the-post (FPTP) system used at the last election A number of candidates have already declared for the contest , triggered after Andy Burnham's successful campaign to become the MP of Makerfield, leaving the post he has held since 2017 vacant Prof Jonathan Tonge of the University of Liverpool said Labour could pick up second preference votes from Greens and Lib Dems, while Reform could be less likely to see Restore voters put them as second preference due to "bad blood" between the party leaders The system was used in the first two mayoral elections, but was scrapped by the last Conservative government in favour of FPTP system, before it was reintroduced again by Labour Under SCV, candidates need to win more than 50% of the vote in the first round to be come mayor or the top two candidates go into a run-off where second preference votes from eliminated candidates are counted

title: What is the Greater Manchester mayor and what do they do?

input.lead: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.summary: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.evidence_text: Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they will be fighting for? The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

title: How do I register to vote in the May 2026 elections?

input.lead: Here’s what you need to know about how you register to vote and when
input.summary: Here’s what you need to know about how you register to vote and when
input.evidence_text: Click to expand How do I register to vote? Millions of voters across England, Scotland and Wales are heading to the polls on 7 May. Depending where you are, you might be voting to elect your local council members, a new mayor, members of the Welsh Senedd, or Scottish Parliament But if you're going to vote, you need to make sure you are registered. The deadline for registering to vote has passed but there's still time to apply for a postal or proxy vote Here's what you need to know about how and when you register to vote Registering only takes a few minutes if you do it online. It’s the same process whether you plan to vote in person at a polling station, by post, or through a proxy voter Go to the gov.uk website and fill out the form. You'll need your National Insurance number and current address details to hand If you can't provide your NI number you can still complete the online form but you'll need to provide documents like a passport or utility bill to your local electoral registration office before you're properly registered

title: Fish and chip shop staff say working in heatwave is 'horrendous'

input.lead: With temperatures soaring, chippy workers describe how they are surviving tough heatwave conditions
input.summary: With temperatures soaring, chippy workers describe how they are surviving tough heatwave conditions
input.evidence_text: Fish and chip shop staff say working in heatwave is 'horrendous' - BBC News Image source, Chippy-T Image caption, Tracy Stock said trade stayed steady at her business Chippy T in Southport, Merseyside By Lynette Horsburgh North West Published 10 July 2026 With heatwave temperatures soaring, the thought of a sizzling hot plate of fish and chips might not be top of everybody's favourite dinner ideas - but spare a thought for the chippy staff keeping a great British tradition alive and well. Here, the BBC asks a range of frazzled fryers across the north-west of England how they are coping as the mercury reaches over 30C (86F). Some have told how they are determined to keep the batter flowing while others have been forced to power down the kitchen and close their doors. Others say delivery orders are booming - as more people opt to stay indoors and order takeaways. Andrew Crook, president of the National Federation of Fish Friers - the official body representing chippies - said the extreme heat is "horrendous" to work in and is even worse for those cooped up in mobile fish and chip vans. "It can be up to 50°C behind the range," he said, even with air conditioning and fans in place. Image caption, Some shops have been forced to close because of the heat The 50-year-old advises staff to drink lots of water and provide drinks for the team with plenty of breaks. Andrew, who owns Skippers in Euxton, Chorley, said he had to cover his manager during the last heatwave as she was struggling too much. He said the soaring temperatures have a "massive" impact on business. Andrew said while chippies on the coastline can be "heaving" as people rush to the seaside, often sales at shops inland can dip by about 30%. title: Train disruption due to hot weather across the North West

input.lead: A number of services have been delayed or cancelled due to hot weather across the region
input.summary: A number of services have been delayed or cancelled due to hot weather across the region
input.evidence_text: A number of rail services across the North West have been cancelled or delayed due to hot temperatures There are no Northern Rail trains running from Manchester Victoria to Liverpool Lime Street or from Manchester Piccadilly to Alderley Edge, among widespread disruption to other services across the region Confirming delays and cancellations, Northern Rail said high temperatures can affect passenger safety by causing issues with overhead lines or causing tracks to buckle More information on changes specific services can be found on the Northern Railway website , external "We are balancing a risk of either an issue with trains or tracks, meaning trains are then stranded," Stuart Taylor Northern Rail service delivery director said "By running a reduced service we minimise the risk of that happening and it enables us to offer a more certain timetable to customers, accepting that it is not to the normal standard we like to deliver."

title: How Geoff Hurst became England's World Cup hat-trick hero

input.summary: In the final minute of extra time, as England battled West Germany in the 1966 World Cup final, Bobby Moore launched a long ball to an unmarked Sir Geoff Hurst The striker from Ashton-under-Lyne was exhausted as the end of the extra time drew near, and decided he was "just going
input.evidence_text: In the final minute of extra time, as England battled West Germany in the 1966 World Cup final, Bobby Moore launched a long ball to an unmarked Sir Geoff Hurst The striker from Ashton-under-Lyne was exhausted as the end of the extra time drew near, and decided he was "just going to hit the ball as hard as I can" "And of course, I mis-hit it, and it flew in", Sir Geoff told BBC Radio 4 in 2018, while looking back at the final goal of his hat-trick in England's now iconic 4-2 victory at Wembley As the Three Lions prepare to face off against Norway later, BBC Radio Manchester has been looking back at Sir Geoff's memories of his path to the final and his era-defining hat-trick 60 years ago Sir Geoff was born in 1941 in Ashton-under-Lyne, where he grew up with his mother Evelyn and father Charlie, who was also a professional footballer Charlie played for Oldham Athletic, Rochdale and Mossley before the family moved South for him to join Chelmsford City

input.evidence_text: Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they will be fighting for? The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

input.evidence_text: Click to expand How do I register to vote? Millions of voters across England, Scotland and Wales are heading to the polls on 7 May. Depending where you are, you might be voting to elect your local council members, a new mayor, members of the Welsh Senedd, or Scottish Parliament But if you're going to vote, you need to make sure you are registered. The deadline for registering to vote has passed but there's still time to apply for a postal or proxy vote Here's what you need to know about how and when you register to vote Registering only takes a few minutes if you do it online. It’s the same process whether you plan to vote in person at a polling station, by post, or through a proxy voter Go to the gov.uk website and fill out the form. You'll need your National Insurance number and current address details to hand If you can't provide your NI number you can still complete the online form but you'll need to provide documents like a passport or utility bill to your local electoral registration office before you're properly registered

Women strolling around the market stalls are fanning themselves and sheltering from the sun under umbrellas, while the juice shops and stalls are doing a roaring t But as temperatures climbed into the 30s, that didn't deter one man from standing on a street corner - like he said he does every Friday - to collect donations for families affected by the conflict in Gaza Waving a Palestinian flag and carrying a donation bucket, many people greet him and shake his hand as they walk past. This is a cause that means a lot to the Muslim community that lives here, and to ones like it across the country. input.evidence_text: Courts Driver accused of murdering teenager 'trying to defend the indefensible', prosecutors claim Terrence King, 60, denies murdering 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines News Andrew Bardsley Court reporter 17:28, 10 Jul 2026 View Image Devon Simmonds-C A driver accused of murdering a teenage boy he hit with his car was accused of ‘trying to defend the indefensible’, a jury has heard Terrence King, 60, denies murdering 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines. Jurors at Minshull Street Crown Court have heard that King has admitted manslaughter Prosecutors alleged that King deliberately hit the boy with his car during an incident on Rowrah Crescent in Middleton on July 22 last year. King denied this and claimed he was trying to ‘block the bike in’, saying he had no intention to harm anyone. At the time of the crash, Dev The e-bike had been ‘robbed’ earlier that day by Devon and another youth, with the owner of the e-bike being ‘physically dragged’ off it, the court has heard. On the third day of the trial, King took to the witness box to give evidence in his defence Jurors heard that during an interview with police, King ‘refused to look at’ the footage of the crash. After being asked why, by defence barrister Mukul Chawla KC, King said: “Because Devon had passed away.”

He threatened to carry out the attack at GMP HQ unless they returned pr In January and February 2024 he also sent a series of threatening emails to professionals, including Salford MP Rebecca Long-Bailey and Sir Robert Buckland, who at the time was MP for South Swindon, as well as a judge, solicitor, chartered surveyor and Nat West bank. McGrath, of Martyn Walsh, prosecuting, said in one email sent to Ms Long-Bailey, McGrath said: "You all messed with the wrong person. My army is much bigger considering I have Russian and Chinese support. We will be armed and shoot to kill." In another message, he told the Labour MP "you are a target" and gave a time when her office would explode. Mr Walsh told the court: "Ms Long-Bailey said the incident 'made me feel worried about the safety of my staff and myself'."

The church has had a long history but after years of being closed, the roof of the chapel partially collapsed in February 2019 Works were carried out in 2019 following the collapse to make the building safer and the Methodist church have been criticised for how they left the historic building. Since then, the building has continued to decay further Plans were put forward to carry out further works to the building. Mohammadia Masjid and Islamic Centre bought the site in 2021 in the hopes of establishing ‘a community social and religious assembly’. Women strolling around the market stalls are fanning themselves and sheltering from the sun under umbrellas, while the juice shops and stalls are doing a roaring t But as temperatures climbed into the 30s, that didn't deter one man from standing on a street corner - like he said he does every Friday - to collect donations for families affected by the conflict in Gaza Waving a Palestinian flag and carrying a donation bucket, many people greet him and shake his hand as they walk past. This is a cause that means a lot to the Muslim community that lives here, and to ones like it across the country. That's a fact that has been borne out by success Dissatisfied with Labour's initial response to the conflict in Gaza that sprang from the Hamas-led October 7 terror attacks in Israel in 2023, many have voted with their feet and turned former Labour strongholds into fertile ground for Green and independent candidates The Longsight ward has lost two Labour councillors to these parties since 2023

However Derbyshire Fire and Rescue has revealed five crews were called back to the scene yesterday at noon after a flare-up in woodland near So far around 2.6 million square metres of moorland has been scorched – the equivalent of more than 350 football pitches. With dry weather and heatwave continuing, a high wildfire warning remains in place across the Peak District this weekend In a statement today DFRS group manager Louise Dempsey said: "On behalf of Derbyshire Fire & Rescue Service, I would like to thank everyone who has supported the response to this incident to date. input.evidence_text: Andy Burnham Hoaxer threatened to bomb GMP headquarters and claimed Andy Burnham was 'protecting paedophiles' Anthony McGrath also threatened Salford MP Rebecca Long-Bailey News Damon Wilkinson Reporter and Eleanor Barlow Press Association 16:59, 10 J A bomb hoaxer threatened to bomb Greater Manchester Police headquarters after accusing Andy Burnham of 'protecting paedophiles', a court has heard Anthony McGrath, 57, posted on social media site X in November, claiming the bombs would arrive from Russia and added: "Stop protecting #paedophiles @AndyBurnhamGM because the town hall will also be removed." He threatened to carry out the attack at GMP HQ unless they returned pr In January and February 2024 he also sent a series of threatening emails to professionals, including Salford MP Rebecca Long-Bailey and Sir Robert Buckland, who at the time was MP for South Swindon, as well as a judge, solicitor, chartered surveyor and Nat West bank. McGrath, of Martyn Walsh, prosecuting, said in one email sent to Ms Long-Bailey, McGrath said: "You all messed with the wrong person. My army is much bigger considering I have Russian and Chinese support. We will be armed and shoot to kill." In another message, he told the Labour MP "you are a target" and gave a time when her office would explode. Mr Walsh told the court: "Ms Long-Bailey said the incident 'made me feel worried about the safety of my staff and myself'."

A court hearing under the Proceeds of Crime Act (POCA) relating to James Bury , 39, has now concluded, detectives who brought him to justice have The court identified £49,133 in available assets, which it ordered to be confiscated. The assets included approximately £2,000 in cryptocurrency recovered by investigators from the North West Regional Organised Crime Unit (NWROCU) Bury, from Westhoughton in Bolton , was jailed for 10 years and six months in June last year after he pleaded guilty to conspiracy to supply Class A drugs and possession with intent to supply controlled drugs He posted out drug deliveries through the mail and was caught after a number of parcels were intercepted in Northern Ireland. Police said Bury 'believed he could exploit the postal system to courier drugs across the UK' Four parcels containing more than 2kg of cocaine were seized by Border Force authorities in Northern Ireland. The church has had a long history but after years of being closed, the roof of the chapel partially collapsed in February 2019 Works were carried out in 2019 following the collapse to make the building safer and the Methodist church have been criticised for how they left the historic building. Mohammadia Masjid and Islamic Centre bought the site in 2021 in the hopes of establishing ‘a community social and religious assembly’. Women strolling around the market stalls are fanning themselves and sheltering from the sun under umbrellas, while the juice shops and stalls are doing a roaring t But as temperatures climbed into the 30s, that didn't deter one man from standing on a street corner - like he said he does every Friday - to collect donations for families affected by the conflict in Gaza Waving a Palestinian flag and carrying a donation bucket, many people greet him and shake his hand as they walk past. This is a cause that means a lot to the Muslim community that lives here, and to ones like it across the country. That's a fact that has been borne out by success Dissatisfied with Labour's initial response to the conflict in Gaza that sprang from the Hamas-led October 7 terror attacks in Israel in 2023, many have voted with their feet and turned former Labour strongholds into fertile ground for Green and independent candidates The Longsight ward has lost two Labour councillors to these parties since 2023

Jurors at Minshull Street Crown Court have heard that King has admitted manslaughter Prosecutors alleged that King deliberately hit the boy with his car during an incident on Rowrah Crescent in Middleton on July 22 last year. King denied this and claimed he was trying to ‘block the bike in’, saying he had no intention to harm anyone. At the time of the crash, Dev The e-bike had been ‘robbed’ earlier that day by Devon and another youth, with the owner of the e-bike being ‘physically dragged’ off it, the court has heard. On the third day of the trial, King took to the witness box to give evidence in his defence Jurors heard that during an interview with police, King ‘refused to look at’ the footage of the crash. After being asked why, by defence barrister Mukul Chawla KC, King said: “Because Devon had passed away.”

He threatened to carry out the attack at GMP HQ unless they returned pr In January and February 2024 he also sent a series of threatening emails to professionals, including Salford MP Rebecca Long-Bailey and Sir Robert Buckland, who at the time was MP for South Swindon, as well as a judge, solicitor, chartered surveyor and Nat West bank. A court hearing under the Proceeds of Crime Act (POCA) relating to James Bury , 39, has now concluded, detectives who brought him to justice have The court identified £49,133 in available assets, which it ordered to be confiscated. The assets included approximately £2,000 in cryptocurrency recovered by investigators from the North West Regional Organised Crime Unit (NWROCU) Bury, from Westhoughton in Bolton , was jailed for 10 years and six months in June last year after he pleaded guilty to conspiracy to supply Class A drugs and possession with intent to supply controlled drugs He posted out drug deliveries through the mail and was caught after a number of parcels were intercepted in Northern Ireland. Police said Bury 'believed he could exploit the postal system to courier drugs across the UK' Four parcels containing more than 2kg of cocaine were seized by Border Force authorities in Northern Ireland. input.summary: National news 'Historic moment' as Bayeux Tapestry returns to UK for first time in 960 years The Bayeux Tapestry returned to the United Kingdom today Comments News PA and Paul Britton Reporter 10:15, 10 Jul 2026 View 3 Images The Bayeux Tapestry (Imag Under the cover of darkness
input.evidence_text: National news 'Historic moment' as Bayeux Tapestry returns to UK for first time in 960 years The Bayeux Tapestry returned to the United Kingdom today Comments News PA and Paul Britton Reporter 10:15, 10 Jul 2026 View 3 Images The Bayeux Tapestry (Imag Under the cover of darkness and a police escort, the Bayeux Tapestry returned to the United Kingdom for the first time in almost 1,000 years on Thursday night Overnight, the 70-metre-long (229ft) tapestry, which is linen with embroidered images of the Norman Conquest, arrived at the British Museum from France. It was created around 960 years ago The Metropolitan Police Service and Kent Police transported the 11th-century embroidery from Folkestone to London in what the British Museum has called “one of the most significant international museum loans ever undertaken between the two countries” Director of the British Museum, Dr Nicholas Cullinan, said: “This has been a monumental effort from colleagues at the British Museum and our partners in the UK and France “Watching the Tapestry arrive at the Museum is a moment I will never forget and I look forward to seeing the exhibition take shape over the coming weeks and welcoming the first visitors through our doors this September."

input.evidence_text: EasyJet has agreed to a takeover by Apollo in a £5.7 billion deal, kickstarting a bidding war for the UK airline which days ago accepted a bid from rival US suitor Castlelake The Luton-based airline said it had reached an agreement in principle for an offer worth £7.15 per easyJet share The offer values easyJet’s fully diluted share capital at about £5.7 billion and would be paid for with a combination of equity and debt This makes it a bigger offer than the £6.90-a-share bid from rival US investment firm Castlelake That offer had valued easyJet’s shares at £5.23 billion, or £5.5 billion on a fully diluted basis EasyJet said Apollo’s offer “delivers a superior outcome” for its shareholders by providing a higher cash value, and the board was therefore “no longer minded to recommend the Castlelake proposal”

input.evidence_text: Salford Family issues major update with man, 20, left in ICU after horror quad bike crash on lads' holiday to Greece The loved ones of Bradley Belhomme may now be able to finally bring him home on a specialised flight News James Holt Senior Live and B The family of a man who was left seriously injured and in a coma after a horror quad bike crash while on a lads' holiday to Greece have issued a major update Bradley Belhomme, from Salford , suffered catastrophic head injuries and was left fighting for his life after the collision with a coach on the trip to Zante , in Greece last Friday (July 3), his loved ones previously said Bradley had flown out to the party hotspot with a group of friends on July 2. The party had then rented quads to ride on when disaster struck and the 20-year-old was involved in the serious crash while riding with another friend on the bike, who was also put in hospital Bradley was temporarily taken to hospital in Zante, before being moved over to an intensive care unit in a hospital in Attica, in Athens, his family said. Over the last week, doctors have attempted to reduce Bradley's sedation and reliance on a ventilator but this has been unsucc His loved ones say they may now be able to finally bring him home, as early as this weekend, but that he needs a specialised repatriation medical flight costing in excess of £40,000

title: The beautiful Mancunian bling made from the history of Manchester Town Hall

input.lead: Manchester City Centre The beautiful Mancunian bling made from the history of Manchester Town Hall 'One-of-a-kind creations' Comments News Paul Britton Reporter 06:25, 10 Jul 2026 View 4 Images A restored stained-glass window at Manchester Town Hall ( The ornate stained-glass win
input.summary: Manchester City Centre The beautiful Mancunian bling made from the history of Manchester Town Hall 'One-of-a-kind creations' Comments News Paul Britton Reporter 06:25, 10 Jul 2026 View 4 Images A restored stained-glass window at Manchester Town Hall ( The ornate stained-glass win
input.evidence_text: Manchester City Centre The beautiful Mancunian bling made from the history of Manchester Town Hall 'One-of-a-kind creations' Comments News Paul Britton Reporter 06:25, 10 Jul 2026 View 4 Images A restored stained-glass window at Manchester Town Hall ( The ornate stained-glass windows of Manchester Town Hall have held many a gaze for almost 150 years. From politicians deep in thought to famous figures from times past, celebrities on film sets - and the public outside. All have peered up at their delicate grandeur Now, as the gothic, Grade I-listed town hall gears ever nearer to opening up to the public again following a mammoth restoration project, the delicate panes of glass have been repurposed and saved - as the ultimate in Mancunian bling A unique collection of jewellery - crafted from original stained-glass salvaged from the windows - is going on sale for charity. From earrings to necklaces, pendants to cufflinks, it's the chance to wear a piece of Manchester's history dating back to 1877 The collection has been created by Derbyshire-based Lin Fletcher, a stained-glass artist and member of a specialist team of contractors. Manchester Town Hall has around 1,800 windows. The restoration involved conservation work to some 4,459 individual stained-glass panels Lin has been working on the windows since early 2021. As part of the project, all of them were removed and their conditions assessed

title: Andy Burnham wins Makerfield by-election: What happens next with Greater Manchester mayoralty?

input.lead: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade.
input.summary: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A
input.evidence_text: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A metro mayor cannot simultaneously serve as an MP, a clear legal and electoral process is now underway to find someone new to take on the role Andy Burnham has won a seat as an MP - but can he still be the Mayor of Greater Manchester? No. Andy Burnham cannot be mayor and a member of parliament. He had seven days - from the moment of his election - to resign from the post which he has held for almost a decade He handed in his resignation on 19 June - triggering the process of the by-election Burnham cannot do both jobs because the Mayor of Greater Manchester holds Police and Crime Commissioner (PCC) powers and UK law strictly dictates that the office holder cannot simultaneously serve as an MP

A year later, we met him in court On the first day of the trial earlier this month, TV historian Laurence Westgaph was asked to turn to page 255 of the hearing bundle: the thick ringbinder of documents that chronicle a year-long legal battle that was now, finally, coming to a head in court Westgaph, a well-known figure in Liverpool who has presented history shows on the BBC, was asked to read a Facebook post he’d published about me in July last year. He began to read it out “I served the big busta, Mr Herrmann this week…” Our barrister, Claire Overman, stopped him. She hadn’t meant for him to read the post out loud. Everyone in court read the Facebook post in silence. “This is just the starter though,” Westgaph had written on top of an image of himself staring at the camera with his shirt unbutton This was the denouement of a tough hour-long cross-examination of Westgaph, in which it often looked like he was struggling to explain why he had initiated this lawsuit in the first place. title: Let's get metaphysical: could an ex-philosopher be the new Burnham?

input.lead: She was one of Greater Manchester's first Green councillors.
input.summary: She was one of Greater Manchester's first Green councillors. Now she has her eyes on a bigger prize
input.evidence_text: She was one of Greater Manchester's first Green councillors. Now she has her eyes on a bigger prize Before politics, one interest dominated the life of Geraldine Coggins, leader of the Trafford Greens, candidate for Greater Manchester mayor, and that was metaphysical nihilism. The now-politician harbours a lifelong love of philosophy, studying the subject up and down the UK and What is metaphysical nihilism? When I ask her, Coggins seems reluctant to discuss it, directing me instead towards the book’s synopsis. “The content is very abstract,” she tells me, “and doesn’t affect my worldview now.” Maybe Coggins is trying to draw a distinction between the r And what a time in the Green Party it’s been. When I meet her, drinking matcha outside a glass-fronted MediaCity cafe, she runs me through the last decade-and-a-half in her Irish lilt: a councillor for eight years, a group leader for four; becoming one of Trafford's first Green c Coggins possesses a demeanour at once warm and authoritative, and it’s this unlikely mix that I’m told helped her win the favour of Altrincham’s electorate in 2018, when, alongside councillor Daniel Jerrome, she made history Two seats were vacant in the ward that year, and the pair stood as the Green candidates. Jerrome recalls a febrile atmosphere on polling day, with neither opposition parties nor the press able to predict what was to come

title: Chezdan was young, gay and living in a council house in Bolton. He also wanted to be on TV — at any cost

input.lead: Aged ten and eight, my brother and I appeared on the Saturday morning horror-comedy show Ross Lee’s Ghoulies .
input.summary: Aged ten and eight, my brother and I appeared on the Saturday morning horror-comedy show Ross Lee’s Ghoulies . Our task was this: we were to dress as zombies and crouch in the bushes at the local golf course while our Dad enjoyed a morning of hooks and slices
input.evidence_text: Aged ten and eight, my brother and I appeared on the Saturday morning horror-comedy show Ross Lee’s Ghoulies . Our task was this: we were to dress as zombies and crouch in the bushes at the local golf course while our Dad enjoyed a morning of hooks and slices. When he reached the There followed the ghoulies, four of them, a Nickelodeon-troupe of pasty white zombies waddling out from the shrubbery, followed by serial prankster and TV host Ross Lee (who would eventually hit dizzying heights with the Keith Lemon Sketch Show). When we got to the studio in Lon Chezdan Mills had his own post-TV fame realisation that same year, 2009. His came at a bus stop The show Chezdan had been on had a viewership more than a touch higher than Ross Lee’s Ghoulies . title: Review: Johnny Marr's electrifying homecoming show at the Castlefield Bowl, Manchester

input.lead: It was the Mancunian music legend's biggest solo show yet Share on WhatsApp Share via Email Fifty years ago, a 12-year-old Johnny Marr - or John Maher as he still was then - would get the bus from Wythenshawe and spend his Saturday mornings in a guitar shop next door to Altrincha
input.summary: It was the Mancunian music legend's biggest solo show yet Share on WhatsApp Share via Email Fifty years ago, a 12-year-old Johnny Marr - or John Maher as he still was then - would get the bus from Wythenshawe and spend his Saturday mornings in a guitar shop next door to Altrincha
input.evidence_text: It was the Mancunian music legend's biggest solo show yet Share on WhatsApp Share via Email Fifty years ago, a 12-year-old Johnny Marr - or John Maher as he still was then - would get the bus from Wythenshawe and spend his Saturday mornings in a guitar shop next door to Altrincham Market. He was too young to be paid, but he was rewarded The 62-year-old Johnny Marr still just wants to be around guitars, but now 8,000 people want to watch him do it. Last night's sold-out gig at the Castlefield Bowl - the fourth of this year's Sounds Of The City series - was significant in being his biggest ever solo show, and what Such was the remarkably prodigious nature of Marr's early work - The Smiths' 70 original songs and four studio albums were all completed before his 24th birthday - that it has inevitably cast a long shadow over a career now well into its fifth decade The intervening period has been testament to a work ethic rooted in his working-class upbringing - alongside session work with Oasis, Pet Shop Boys, Talking Heads and many more, he has written a US number 1 album with Modest Mouse, scored Oscar-nominated film soundtracks with Han But for all the times he has sprinkled his genius on others, the past 13 years have also been about Johnny Marr the solo artist and his fifth studio album, The Age Of Everything, arrives in October

input.evidence_text: The incident happened on Bradgate Road last month Share on WhatsApp Share via Email A convicted murderer has pleaded guilty to kidnapping a 15-year-old girl at knifepoint and subjecting her to an eight-hour ordeal in his car Paul Sharpe, 56, of Portwood, Stockport, entered guilty pleas at Minshull Street Crown Court on Thursday to six offences: kidnapping, sexual assault, engaging in sexual activity in the presence of a child, making threats with a bladed article, committing an offence with intent to Sharpe approached the girl on Bradgate Road in Altrincham at around 7.30pm on May 18, threatened her with a knife, put her in a headlock and forced her into his car. He told her that her father owed him money He drove her to a car park near Manchester Airport, where he tied her wrists and ankles with a necktie. Over the following hours he moved between different car parks, forcing her to drink alcohol and sexually assaulting her The girl’s mother reported her missing, prompting a major police investigation. Extensive CCTV enquiries identified Sharpe’s vehicle, and he was arrested in a hotel car park in Knutsford at around 3.40am the following morning with the girl still in the car

title: New outdoor basketball court opens in Cringle Park as part of city-wide investment in community sport

input.lead: Manchester City Council, in partnership with Manchester Basketball and the National Governing Body Basketball England, has completed a major refurbishment of the outdoor basketball court at Cringle Park in Levenshulme

input.evidence_text: Manchester City Council, in partnership with Manchester Basketball and the National Governing Body Basketball England, has completed a major refurbishment of the outdoor basketball court at Cringle Park in Levenshulme The project has seen the existing court fully transformed, including a deep clean, a brand-new playing surface, updated posts and hoops, and a vibrant new colour design to create a high-quality and welcoming space for local residents The investment is part of a wider programme to improve community sport facilities across Manchester. Work is also scheduled to take place at Eggington Street Park in Harpurhey, where two courts will be overhauled ahead of the summer The programme is supported by a £47,000 investment from Manchester City Council and Manchester Basketball, alongside a contribution from Basketball England The new courts will be supported by a programme of community activity and activation sessions, including opportunities for local young people to engage with players from Manchester Basketball The city is also home to Basketball England and hosts a wide range of programmes, from junior leagues to inclusive sessions for disabled and SEN participants

From the rising cost of energy to food and housing, we want to make sure that residents know where to go to get friendly and helpful support whatever they are going through Nationally, almost half of those who come to Citizens Advice about debt are already at the point of crisis when they first make contact. input.evidence_text: ‘Your future has no limits’ is the inspiring message being shared with young people with additional needs and disabilities across the borough of Rochdale This follows a careers event organised by Rochdale’s additional needs service (RANS), part of Rochdale Borough Council, at Middleton Arena to inspire the career aspirations of local pupils aged 10-14 with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) During a day packed full of careers advice, more than 60 pupils from nearby schools had the opportunity to hear from a number of successful professionals with additional needs This included 2-time Paralympic gold medallist and double amputee runner, Richard Whitehead MBE, who also holds the world record for the fastest marathon by a leg amputee This event came as national figures highlight only half the number of people in the UK with a disability aged 16-64 are in employment (52.8%), compared to 4 out of 5 people (82.5%) who don’t have a disability Local services have been working hard however, to address this challenge by highlighting the successful careers and futures that young people can achieve with a disability as they grow up

title: More than a game: football's role in one family's fostering journey - Rochdale Borough Council

input.lead: A foster family from Heywood have opened up about how their love for football has helped to create the ultimate loving environment for the children in their care
input.summary: A foster family from Heywood have opened up about how their love for football has helped to create the ultimate loving environment for the children in their care
input.evidence_text: A foster family from Heywood have opened up about how their love for football has helped to create the ultimate loving environment for the children in their care Lauren (46) and her husband Danny Sullivan (52) have fostered for Rochdale Borough Council since 2012 and currently care for 5 young lads aged 12 to 21, alongside their 9-year-old son, Daniel Their home is very busy as you can imagine and ‘the washing machine is non-stop’ Lauren jokes. Each child has their own interests, including things like the gym and gaming, but football is what stands out as the cement in their daily routines From training and coaching on weekdays to refereeing and playing matches at the weekend, the house is football mad and it’s their passion for the sport which connects them as a family and what has helped the lads to create a strong brotherly bond “They’ll all come home after school, college or work, and Daniel will ask them for a game,” Lauren said, with a pair of football nets visible outside from the living room “It’s so lovely to see the smiles on their faces when they’re all playing together in the back garden.”

title: New funding will help the council tackle the sticky issue of chewing gum on Rochdale's streets - Rochdale Borough Council

input.lead: Rochdale Borough Council has been awarded a grant from the Chewing Gum Task Force, administered by environmental charity Keep Britain Tidy, to help clean up gum and reduce gum littering
input.summary: Rochdale Borough Council has been awarded a grant from the Chewing Gum Task Force, administered by environmental charity Keep Britain Tidy, to help clean up gum and reduce gum littering
input.evidence_text: Rochdale Borough Council has been awarded a grant from the Chewing Gum Task Force, administered by environmental charity Keep Britain Tidy, to help clean up gum and reduce gum littering Today, the council announced plans to step up the removal of chewing gum that blights Rochdale town centre after receiving a £27,461 grant to tackle the issue Rochdale is one of 50 councils across the country that have successfully applied to the Chewing Gum Task Force, now in its fifth year, for funds to clean gum off pavements and prevent it from being littered in the first place The Chewing Gum Task Force grant scheme – established by Defra (Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs) and run by environmental charity Keep Britain Tidy – is open to councils across the UK who wish to clean up gum in their local areas and invest in long-term behavio The Task Force is funded by major gum manufacturers including Mars Wrigley and Perfetti Van Melle, with an investment of up to £10 million spread over 5 years In the past 4 years, the Task Force has awarded grants worth a total of £6.46 million, funded the cleaning of over 4.15 million square metres of pavements

input.evidence_text: Plantation Lodges and Gates have opened following an extensive renovation period, funded by the National Lottery Heritage Fund and Wigan Council The iconic Grade II* listed Lodges and Gates, located on Wigan Lane at the edge of Haigh Woodland Park, have been fully restored over an 18 month period by Wigan Council and were officially opened by Lisa Nandy MP, Secretary of state for culture, media and sport, on Friday, 3rd J Speaking at the event, Ms Nandy said: “This is Wigan, we are friendly, welcoming people who believe these assets are for us all forever. This is more than a beautiful restoration, it’s a bridge between our past, present and future. Places like Haigh bring us together.’ The opening was also attended by The Mayor of Wigan Borough, Councillor Anne Collins, and Councillor Nazia Rehman, Leader of Wigan Council Also in attendance was Councillor Chris Ready, portfolio holder for communities and neighbourhoods. Councillor Ready said: "It’s incredible to have Plantation Gates and Lodges restored and open to residents for the very first time. The reaction to the transformation and the artwo Also in attendance was David Woods, a former resident who lived in the Lodges with his family over 50 years ago. input.evidence_text: Wigan’s new market will officially open next Spring following engagement with traders Wigan Council met with trader representatives yesterday (Tuesday 7 July) to provide an update on the new market, including how it will operate once open Wigan Market at Fettlers will open in Spring 2027, allowing time for the council and traders to shape a venue that supports existing businesses and offers the best possible visitor experience in the town centre. The timetable also means traders will have the opportunity to enjoy Following the decision for the market to be operated directly by Wigan Council, additional work is now being undertaken to focus on developing an operating model that works for the people at the heart of the market This insight is now feeding into the final layout of the building’s internal design, ensuring the market’s long-standing businesses remain at the heart of one of the borough’s most significant regeneration projects Wigan Market forms the centrepiece of the wider Fettlers development and will bring the market back to the heart of Wigan town centre, creating a vibrant destination where people can shop, eat, meet and spend time together

title: Encouraging decline in youth smoking and vaping across Wigan Borough

input.lead: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.summary: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.evidence_text: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally and protect the health of our children and young people With more than 1,000 school children aged 14-17 included in the Trading Standards survey, the findings show the number of young people who have tried vaping dropped from around 44 per cent in 2023 to 18 per cent in 2026 The survey, carried out between November 2025 and February 2026, also highlights that smoking is at an all-time low, with around 97 per cent reporting to have never tried smoking Councillor Keith Cunliffe, Deputy Leader of Wigan Council and cabinet portfolio holder for adult social care and health, said: “I’m really pleased to see smoking and vaping rates among young people in Wigan Borough continuing to fall “Reducing tobacco and nicotine use among young people is one of the most effective ways to prevent long-term ill health and reduce health inequalities in our communities

input.evidence_text: The Mayor unveils the new Blue Plaque for Dr John Garton on Park Road, Golborne A renowned Wigan Borough scientist has received a heritage Blue Plaque outside his former home in Golborne Dr John Garton’s (1863–1922) pioneering work in crop manufacturing has had a lasting impact across the globe The Blue Plaque was unveiled at a ceremony on Park Road, Golborne, attended by the Mayor of Wigan Borough Councillor Chris Ready, cabinet portfolio holder for communities and neighbourhoods, said: “Dr John Garton’s work has helped transform farming practices and improve sustainability, making him a significant figure not just locally, but across the world “The Blue Plaque scheme is designed to celebrate the impact residents of our borough have made and Dr Garton’s legacy and influence in the world of agriculture will live on.”

input.evidence_text: Wigan Council will commit £3m to a flood defence scheme that could unlock further major funding Ahead of public consultation this summer, the local authority is hoping its contribution will attract wider support The scheme would see flood risk prevention measures tailored for communities in Hindley, Platt Bridge and Abram, areas recently affected by river flooding The consultation will provide an opportunity to assess the available options and to learn more about how the projects will progress Councillor Nazia Rehman, Leader of Wigan Council, said: “This commitment sets out a clear timeline for how we will support our residents, many of whom have been impacted by recent flooding “We have been consistently making the case on their behalf; listening to residents, working closely with partners such as Defra and the Environment Agency, and lobbying for the funding and support our communities need

input.evidence_text: Inhale Me, Exhale You By Kate Partington Tue 2 Jun 10:00 - Mon 28 Sep 22:00 Art Tue 2 Jun - Mon 28 Sep From 10:00 Tue 2 Jun - Mon 28 Sep From 10:00 Arches Windows No booking required Unspoken feelings and moments of desire You can see this artwork in HOME Arches Windows on Whitwo This piece belongs to a wider series called ‘Desire Me.’ It aims to translate unspoken feelings and moments of desire that occur between us The focus is on longing, on reaching out and on instances of hopeful touch that happen in the early stages of a relationship. title: Marina Abramović: Balkan Erotic Epic | Aviva Studios

input.lead: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović .
input.summary: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance a
input.evidence_text: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance artist, Abramović’s transformative work has reached millions of people – from opening the Pyramid Stage at Glastonbury Festival t A four-hour ritual where ancient myth meets performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic explores the eroticism, spirituality and traditions of Abramović’s homeland through 13 visceral scenes Brought to life by a cast of over 70 performers, dancers, musicians and singers, the experience unfolds across Aviva Studios. Audiences are free to choose their own path – with pop-up encounters punctuating the action, bringing bodies together in dance, song and ritual Watch Fertility Rite , a fevered ritual where bodies writhe against the ground in a desperate call for fertility. Witness Massaging the Breast where women gesticulate over graves to awaken the earth. In Scaring the Gods , women bare themselves to the sky to banish storms. Listen Unafraid to push the boundaries of eroticism and performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic asks its audience a timeless question: What are our bodies truly for?

title: Manchester Vocal Ensemble

input.lead: Manchester Vocal Ensemble Sun 19 Jul 2026 3:00PM Music Theatre Sun 19 Jul 2026 3:00PM Quays Theatre Band A Standard £27.50 Restricted View Standard £27.50 Wheelchair Standard £23.50 From £23.50 Tickets "From" price includes £3.50 transaction fee that will be added to your booking

input.evidence_text: Manchester Vocal Ensemble | Lowry To the main content To the footer Manchester Vocal Ensemble Sun 19 Jul 2026 3:00PM Music Theatre Sun 19 Jul 2026 3:00PM Quays Theatre Band A Standard £27.50 Restricted View Standard £27.50 Wheelchair Standard £23.50 From £23.50 Tickets "From" price includes £3.50 transaction fee that will be added to your booking Experience the power of voices like you’ve never heard before. Join Manchester Vocal Ensemble, one of the city’s most exciting vocal groups, for an electrifying afternoon where iconic songs are transformed through spine-tingling vocal harmony. From Adele and Fleetwood Mac to Hozier and Queen, hear legendary artists reimagined through rich, layered harmonies and a beautifully blended sound. Each performance is crafted to captivate, uplift and surprise — turning familiar hits into something thrillingly new. This collective of outstanding singers brings a bold creative vision to contemporary music, performing completely original arrangements that reveal new colours, textures and emotion in the songs you love. Expect powerful anthems, breathtaking ballads and unforgettable musical moments as we take you on a journey from chart-topping pop to the magic of film and theatre. Whether you love great pop music, stunning vocal harmony, or simply a brilliant live night out, this is a show not to miss. The Ensemble performs with such energy and joyous enthusiasm, with the more tender ballads proving all the more effective by contrast. - The Arts Shelf Discover the artists > Plan Your Visit > Access > Others also viewed Skip Uncovering Ancient Egypt with Bettany Hughes Sun 15 Nov 2026 7:30PM Dates & tickets Natalie Reckert: Songs for an Unwritten Sky Fri 09 Oct 2026 and Sat 10 Oct 2026 Dates & tickets It Walks Around The House At Night Tue 0

Hear from historians and authors Chris Hall and Dr Richard Baxell about the Watch a film curated by Marshall Mateer from the International Brigade Memorial Trust, and learn about the soundtrack to the war from Melanie Hall, a member of local harmony group The Mad Donnas, along with a performance of popular Spanish Civil War songs Explore rare archival material from the museum’s collection, specially selected by the International Brigade Memorial Trust and Manchester Cervantes Institute , including photographs, newspapers, magazines, correspondence, and minutes from the period Meet author Chris Hall who will be signing his new book ‘British Volunteers and the Spanish Civil War: ‘The Passionate Cause,’ 1936-39′ (2026), available to purchase at the event Dr Richard Baxell Historian and author, and former Research Fellow at the London School of Economics’ Cañada Blanch Centre, currently the historical consultant for the International Brigade Memorial Trust. input.evidence_text: Join this creative workshop for some stitching, chatting, and sharing inspired by the museum’s collections What is the right to strike and is it under threat? In this workshop we will visit the On The Line: 100 years of strikes & solidarity exhibition to explore how workers first secured the right to strike in the 1800s, how strike protections have changed and how that has impacted us Participants will create textile responses to the discussions, which will be used as part of a collective textile piece in future workshops Part of PHM’s programme of activity exploring the history of strikes and solidarity Suitable for 12+ (under 16s must have an accompanying adult) If you require British Sign Language (BSL) interpretation please contact access@phm.org.uk or 0161 838 9190

title: Young Musician's Summer School

input.evidence_text: Young Musician’s Summer School . Young Musician’s Summer School. . Book here . . . . . . . Friday | 03.07.2026 . . . World of Music . Share . . . . --> . . We’re inviting young people to explore music for film and compose their own soundtrack this summer! Whether your child already attends our Saturday Music Club , or just wants to try something new, our Young Musician’s Summer School is suitable for all abilities! . . . . . Across this 5 day course, we’ll be exploring music for film, and working on composing music for the silent film ‘A Trip to the Moon’. The course will conclude with a live performance soundtracking the film for family and friends. . . . . We welcome all abilities and instruments to take part in this course. You can bring your own instruments, but we have keyboards, guitars, bass, violins, drums and percussion on site which can be used if you don’t have an instrument.. . . Snacks and drinks provided, and pizzas on the final day. Please bring your own packed lunch to sessions Monday-Thursday.. . Dates: Monday 17th – Friday 21st August. Times: 10:00-16:30 Monday-Thursday, then a performance for friends and family on Friday at 13:00 (students will be expected to come in at 10:00 sharp on the Friday).. Ages: 12-18 years old. Cost: £150 for the full course. Bursaries are available for those on lower income – please email learning@bandonthewall.org to arrange this.. . Spaces are limited – Book here . . . Please email learning@bandonthewall.org if you have any questions about this course

input.evidence_text: MANCHESTER JAZZ FESTIVAL AT BAND ON THE… From fusion pioneers and soul-jazz stars, to high energy afro-jazz and even a club night of global grooves – Explore our full programme for Manchester Jazz Festival below Individual gig tickets are on sale now, plus free shows in The Copper Bar Releasing their eponymous debut album in 1981 at the height of the fusion era, Yellowjackets draw upon a history-spanning mélange of jazz movements, steeped in funk grooves and chamber music sophistication, Yellowjackets have evolved to become a genre unto themselves Signed to Brownswood recordings, enigmatic East London songstress Bel Cobain merges jazz, r&b and rap with a peaceful and self-reflective slant, to create boundary-transcending music powered by radical ethos, poetic lyrics, and ethereal melodies. For fans of: Greentea Peng, ELIZA Somewhere Soul presents Cotonete – Sat 23 May | TICKETS

title: The Only King

input.lead: The Only King is acclaimed as the first original Cantonese biblical musical to present the story of the Book of 1 Samuel in its entirety.
input.summary: The Only King is acclaimed as the first original Cantonese biblical musical to present the story of the Book of 1 Samuel in its entirety. The production follows the life of King Saul, from his anointing and rise to the throne to his gradual downfall through pride and disobedience
input.evidence_text: The Only King is acclaimed as the first original Cantonese biblical musical to present the story of the Book of 1 Samuel in its entirety. The production follows the life of King Saul, from his anointing and rise to the throne to his gradual downfall through pride and disobedience First staged in 2005, The Only King enjoyed multiple sold-out performances in Hong Kong and is widely regarded as a landmark work in Chinese Christian musical theatre To mark the 20th anniversary of its premiere, The Only King was presented in London and Cambridge in January 2026, where it received enthusiastic responses and strong audience acclaim. Building on this success, the production will embark on a UK tour in September 2026, with perfo Performed in Cantonese with English subtitles Suitable for audiences aged 3 and above. Children under the age of 12 must be accompanied by an adult * These tickets include an exclusive souvenir

input.evidence_text: From Prestwich to the city centre, it's a new chapter for Dani Heron and Sofie Stoermann-Naess Go to Manchester's Finest X profile OSMA is set to return to Manchester this autumn, with founders Danielle Heron and Sofie Stoermann-Naess bringing the restaurant back in a new city centre location alongside an ambitious new grocery concept The restaurant will reopen at One Port Street in the Northern Quarter, almost two years after closing its original Prestwich site. The move marks a new chapter for the pair, who will also launch Boutikk, a neighbouring provisions store inspired by Scandinavian food culture and tr Originally opened in 2020, OSMA quickly established itself as one of Greater Manchester’s most acclaimed neighbourhood restaurants. Named after a combination of Oslo and Manchester, the restaurant blended Stoermann-Naess’ Norwegian heritage with Heron’s classical culinary trainin The return will see Heron resume her role as Executive Chef, with a menu centred around seasonal produce, fresh seafood, local meats and vegetable-led dishes. A number of OSMA’s most popular plates are expected to make a comeback, alongside the restaurant’s well-known sharing Sun While the restaurant occupies one side of the development, Boutikk will introduce a new offering to the city centre. The grocery and provisions shop will stock seasonal fruit and vegetables, dairy products, fresh meat and fish, with chefs available to advise customers on ingredie

input.summary: The £35 million project set within 154 acres has transformed the former site of Worsley New Hall, restoring the historic walled garden, lake and surviving buildings Originally built for the 1st Earl of Ellesmere between 1840 and 1845, Worsley New Hall was a grand Gothic-style man
input.evidence_text: The £35 million project set within 154 acres has transformed the former site of Worsley New Hall, restoring the historic walled garden, lake and surviving buildings Originally built for the 1st Earl of Ellesmere between 1840 and 1845, Worsley New Hall was a grand Gothic-style mansion, complete with glorious, formal landscaped gardens It’s now one of UK’s must-see gardens with so much to enjoy for visitors of all ages Step inside the magnificent 11-acre Weston Walled Garden, the site’s jewel in the crown. One of the largest Victorian walled gardens in the UK, it showcases a range of growing techniques and edible plants There’s also the spectacular Paradise Garden showcasing exotic planting inspired by Asiatic and Mediterranean gardens. The orchard and nearby bee and butterfly garden, have been designed to attract wildlife The Peel Learning Garden, supported by The Peel Group, will help schoolchildren learn how plants affect our lives and the planet, whilst the Community Wellbeing Garden provides a space for people to garden, socialise and relax

input.evidence_text: Stained glass salvaged from Manchester Town Hall has been transformed into a unique jewellery collection The Town Hall Jewellery collection has been created by Lin Fletcher, a stained-glass artist and one of the expert team tasked with repairing and restoring more than 4,000 windows as part of the Manchester Town Hall restoration project The jewellery is now for sale at the Manchester Central Library shop with all the profits going to the Lord Mayor’s Charity. Each item in the collection, which includes earrings, pendants, and cufflinks, is a one-of-a-kind creation, complete with flaws and imperfections in the gl As part of the Town Hall renovation, windows were removed, and their condition was assessed. Much of the original glass was still in good condition; this was cleaned and reset with new leading Where it couldn’t be reused, the weakened or damaged pieces of original glass were replaced with new sections. This authentic replacement glas s (offcuts from the new glass that was brought in to replace what couldn’t be reused) was cut to shape as required and added to complete Lin recognised that these small offcuts of original and authentic glass, although unusable in the windows, had wonderful heritage value, and, as a jeweller, saw the opportunity to transform the fragments into something new

input.summary: An imagined anarchic society built around art and community UNITOM Projects , Castlefield Until 11 July 2026 Additional information Always double check opening hours with the venue before making a special visit We love to see a new gallery opening in Manchester, especially one th

UNITOM Projects – the new exhibition arm of UNITOM, the visual culture bookshop – launches in St John’s with a solo show by recent Manchester The 21-year-old painter’s work circles the Beat Generation: its street-level energy, its conviction that art and community and laughter matter more than productivity, its mythology of the city as a place of creative possibility Goof City takes its title from a concept coined by poet and activist Ed Sanders – introduced in his 1963 Poem from Jail – describing an imagined anarchic society built around art and community, historically associated with New York’s East Village. input.summary: Welcome to Manchester Brick Festival! A LEGO fan event dedicated to all things brick!
input.evidence_text: Manchester Brick Festival - Brick Festival Manchester Brick Festival July 11 @ 10:00 am - 4:00 pm Welcome to the Manchester Brick Festival! A LEGO fan event dedicated to all things brick! This will be a great day for families, collectors, adult builders and every other kind of LEGO fan. With lots to see and do including speed building competitions, large scale displays, activities, traders selling loose bricks, new LEGO sets, retired LEGO sets, minifigures & accessories plus a dedicated building area. Established in 2018, this celebration of all things LEGO is open to fans of all ages. Booking tickets to secure your place is strongly advised as we regularly sell out. Early Bird tickets are also available giving you an hour of extra fun and a FREE LEGO gift on entry too! The Manchester Brick Festival will take place on Saturday, 11 July 2026 at Sugden Sports Centre . Buy Tickets Buy Tickets Tickets Early Bird – including free LEGO gift, and entry from 10am: £13 Adult – entry from 11am: £8 Child (5-12) – entry from 11am: £6 Family (2 Adults & 2 Children) – entry from 11am: £20 Under 5s – Free (with a paying adult, no additional ticket required) Buy Tickets Buy Tickets Displays There will be some truly out of this world displays at the event that you won’t want to miss! Each Brick Festival event offers a totally unique selection of great displays. At previous events we have had an incredible large scale Mos Eisley Spaceport diorama from Star Wars, a huge Great Ball Contraption that’s over 30-foot-long, and so many other incredible displays! We’re always looking for exciting builds to feature at Brick Festival events. title: Bolton Car Boot Sale

input.lead: Bolton Car Boot Sale at Bolton Car Boot Sale
input.summary: Huge Sunday car boot at Bolton's Macron Stadium (BL6 6JW), Feb-Dec. 300+ stalls, £1 per car entry, catering, toilets and dog-friendly Event date: 2026-07-12 Venue: Bolton Car Boot Sale Price: £1 Booking: https://carbootdirectory.co.uk/listings/bolton-car-boot/ Status: https://sch
input.evidence_text: Bolton Car Boot Sale | Bolton Sunday Car Boot Sale | Times, Prices & Info Skip to content 210+ UK car boot sales — find one near you this weekend Type your postcode or town to find sales near you Search car boot sales About This Car Boot Sale Bolton Car Boot Sale is one of the North West's biggest weekly boot sales, held in the hardstanding car park of the Macron Stadium (formerly Reebok), Burnden Way, Horwich, Bolton BL6 6JW. It runs every Sunday from February right through to December, drawing over 300 sellers and thousands of bargain hunters each week. Sellers can arrive and set up from 6:00 AM, with buyers welcomed in from 7:00 AM. Entry costs just £1 per car for buyers. Pitches for sellers are £14 for a car, £20 for a van and £3 for a trailer, with regular and corner pitches available on request. Bring exact change to keep the queue moving, and call organiser Andrew on 07470784644 for more details. Expect hundreds of stalls across the concrete hardstanding pitches, so it goes ahead in most weather. On-site you'll find a 5-star catering service, doughnuts and coffee, ice cream and desserts, a meat auction, fresh fruit and veg, plants, and retro sweet stalls. input.evidence_text: Credit: Laurie Lynn Stark Castlefield Bowl, Rice St, Manchester M3 4JR. 12 July 2026 Original Sex Pistols members Steve Jones, Paul Cook , and Glen Matlock will play a major outdoor headline show at Castlefield Bowl, with frontman Frank Carter . The gig will mark exactly 50 years since the punk band’s historic 1976 performance at Manchester’s Lesser Free Trade Hall . Dr John Cooper Clarke and The Undertones will open the night as special guests, a rare occasion when fans will hear classic punk tracks live during this massive anniversary date in the city. title: It Walks Around The House At Night

input.lead: It Walks Around The House At Night
input.summary: A ghost story by Tim Foley · Directed by Neil Bettles Following a sell-out UK tour and London run, the award-winning ThickSkin present this chilling new play by Tim Foley (former Bruntwood Prize-winner) When an out-of-work actor takes a job playing a ghost at an old countryside m
input.evidence_text: A ghost story by Tim Foley · Directed by Neil Bettles Following a sell-out UK tour and London run, the award-winning ThickSkin present this chilling new play by Tim Foley (former Bruntwood Prize-winner) When an out-of-work actor takes a job playing a ghost at an old countryside manor, he expects nothing more than a few cheap scares. But as night after night unfolds, the role begins to blur with reality — and something far more sinister starts to emerge Part ghost story, part psychological thriller, It Walks Around The House At Night draws audiences into a world of creeping dread, unexpected twists and dark humour. A haunted house story for a new era, brought to life through inventive staging, immersive sound and bold visual eff ThickSkin, one of the UK’s most exciting touring companies, joins forces with Tim Foley (award-winning writer of Doctor Who audio dramas; Electric Rosary, Royal Exchange Theatre) to create this thrilling production. Directed by Neil Bettles (ThickSkin’s How Not To Drown, Disney’s Unsettling, darkly comic and full of surprises, the stage is set… for terror! Age Recommendation: 14+ Content Warnings: Loud and sudden noises, haze, strobe, flashing lights, occasional strong language, horror imagery and jump scares. Runtime: 90 minutes (no interval)

title: Андрей Кравченко

input.lead: Андрей Кравченко at Brixton Jamm
input.summary: Андрей Кравченко в Лондоне! 17 октября 2026 года в Brixton Jamm состоится концерт Андрея Кравченко в Лондоне
input.evidence_text: 17 октября 2026 года в Brixton Jamm состоится концерт Андрея Кравченко в Лондоне Андрей Кравченко покорил сердца тысяч слушателей благодаря своему уникальному голосу, харизме и способности превращать личные истории в песни, близкие каждому. В его творчестве смешиваются романтическая лирика, современное эстрадное звучание и проникновенная искренность После серии аншлаговых выступлений на самых престижных сценах Украины, среди которых Жовтневый Дворец и Дворец “Украина”, певец продолжает свое концертное путешествие и приглашает поклонников на особую встречу в Лондоне Концерты Андрея Кравченко известны своей теплой атмосферой: здесь нет дистанции между сценой и залом, ведь каждое выступление превращается в откровенный разговор на языке музыки. Гостей ждут любимые хиты, живой звук, море эмоций и моменты, которые захочется сохранить в памяти над Важной частью творческой миссии артиста остается поддержка Украины — часть средств от концерта будет передана на помощь ВСУ! Подарите себе вечер настоящей романтики, красивой музыки и искренних чувств вместе с Андреем Кравченко

input.evidence_text: Дронотур Бумбокса Європою. Листопад-грудень 2026 року. Презентація максі-синглу "Живий" Кожного разу, коли перед менеджментом гурту Бумбокс стоїть завдання написати пресреліз, ми починаємо згадувати цікаві факти. Скільки концертів зіграли, скільки грошей зібрали, які пісні видали. Це база. Непогано ще пам’ятати, чи був гурт у країні, до якої ми збираємося, чи це пер О, як би хотілося написати: "Концерт гурту Бумбокс" — і як у Львові за добу продано 8000 квитків. Так-так, ми не про Львів розповідаємо, хоча теж про Європу Наші віддані слухачі, якщо ви перебуваєте за кордоном, сподіваємось, вам буде цікаво дізнатися, що після традиційної перерви на декілька місяців, Бумбокс поїде в турне від Ізраїлю до Німеччини, від Англії до Польщі, від Ірландії до Словаччини та Чехії І повеземо ми в це турне свіженький, як херсонський кавун, максі-сингл “Живий”, підсилений піснями з різних альбомів, під частину яких виросли ви, а під частину зростають ваші діти Тож ознайомтеся, будь ласка, уважно з розкладом туру, купуйте квитки заздалегідь, готуйте гроші на донати та мерч — зустрінемося в листопаді
